title: Love Island USA Vasana Montgomery Addresses Racial Slur Use

blocks.content: Vasana Montgomery, the first person removed from the new season of Love Island USA, issued an apology for using a racial slur in past videos. “I want to address a couple videos from my teen years that have recently resurfaced. In those videos, I used a racial slur. There is no excuse for it, and I am deeply sorry. I am embarrassed and disappointed by my words,” Montgomery, who is 25, wrote in her Instagram Story on June 3. “I take full responsibility for what I said and understand why it has hurt and upset people. Since then, I have grown a lot as a person and taken the time to educate myself, listen, learn, and better understand the impact that language can have.” Montgomery was part of the original 12 Islanders, and her removal meant that Gabriel Vasconcelos was rerouted from the OG cast to the first bombshell of the season.

In the resurfaced videos, Montgomery uses the N-word while singing along to a song and playing an arcade game. The videos were not public at the time of her casting. Her firing follows two departures from last season: Yulissa Escobar, who was removed by day two, for a resurfaced podcast where she used the N-word, and Cierra Ortega, who was removed later in the season for using slurs against Asian people online.
